body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Archivist compiled-inference proxy
body.description:
**What changed:** Archivist device owns the compiled-inference proxy; every inference call is intercepted, graph-checked, and fan-out to learning pipeline on miss.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Proxy latency added to every inference call — even always-miss graph checks have overhead; callers may time out if proxy is slow to start
- Epistemic/emotional encoding boundary erodes — someone adds an emotional salience field to knowledge graph 'because it seemed useful'
- Archivist becomes a single point of failure — if it crashes, all inference stalls rather than degrading gracefully
- Learning queue grows unbounded if overnight processing falls behind — disk pressure
- Caller API 'unchanged' assumption breaks when a caller imports inference directly, bypassing proxy

**Signals to watch:**
- PROXY_INTERCEPT log latency percentiles (p99 should stay <100ms)
- Knowledge graph schema drift (grep for emotional_salience, valence, distress in archivist tables)
- Channel: PROXY_FALLBACK messages (proxy down → direct inference bypass)
- Learning queue depth metric (should drain overnight)

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-15 or when T-archivist-device closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** Observation ticket — done when each predicted effect is annotated
